title: Insert multiple rows
summary: Learn how to insert multiple rows into a table in a single SQL statement using the inventory_ops dataset.

sketchBlocks.title: Why insert multiple rows at once?
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
When you need to add several new items to a table, it's often more efficient to insert them all in a single SQL statement. This reduces the number of commands sent to the database and can make your scripts easier to read and maintain.

For example, to add two new inventory items at once:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES
  ('Keychain', 'Accessories', 2.99, 'active', 0, NULL, '2026-02-10'),
  ('Coffee Thermos', 'Kitchen', 18.00, 'draft', 0, 'New arrival', '2026-02-10');
```

This statement adds both items in a single operation.

sketchBlocks.title: Syntax for inserting multiple rows
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
The general syntax for inserting multiple rows is:

```sql
INSERT INTO table_name (column1, column2, ...)
VALUES
  (value1a, value2a, ...),
  (value1b, value2b, ...),
  ...;
```

Each set of parentheses represents a new row. All rows must match the column list in order and type.

sketchBlocks.title: Checking your inserts
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
After inserting multiple rows, you can verify the changes by running a SELECT query:

```sql
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE name IN ('Keychain', 'Coffee Thermos');
```

This will show the new rows you just added.

quizDraft.title: Insert two new inventory items in one statement
quizDraft.prompt:
Write an SQL statement to insert two new rows into the inventory_items table. Add the following items:

- 'Desk Lamp', category 'Decor', price 22.50, status 'active', is_test 0, notes NULL, last_updated '2026-02-10'
- 'Lunch Box', category 'Kitchen', price 8.75, status 'draft', is_test 0, notes 'Seasonal item', last_updated '2026-02-10'

Use a single INSERT statement and specify all columns except 'id'.
quizDraft.hint: List all columns except 'id' and use VALUES with two sets of parentheses.

help.concept: You can insert multiple rows by separating each row's values with a comma in the VALUES clause.
help.hint_1: Start with INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated).
help.hint_2: Each row's values go in parentheses, separated by commas. Make sure to match the column order.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES
  ('Desk Lamp', 'Decor', 22.50, 'active', 0, NULL, '2026-02-10'),
  ('Lunch Box', 'Kitchen', 8.75, 'draft', 0, 'Seasonal item', '2026-02-10');

quizDraft.title: Insert multiple rows with different notes
quizDraft.prompt:
Insert two new items into inventory_items:

- 'Travel Mug', category 'Kitchen', price 12.00, status 'active', is_test 0, notes 'Popular item', last_updated '2026-02-11'
- 'Wall Calendar', category 'Decor', price 6.50, status 'active', is_test 0, notes NULL, last_updated '2026-02-11'

Write a single SQL statement for both rows, specifying all columns except 'id'.
quizDraft.hint: Remember to use NULL for notes where needed, and separate the two rows with a comma.

help.concept: Each row's values must match the column order and type. Use NULL for missing values.
help.hint_1: Write INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated) VALUES (...), (...);
help.hint_2: For the second row, notes should be NULL. Don't forget the comma between the two sets of values.

quizDraft.starterCode: -- Insert both items in one statement
quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES
  ('Travel Mug', 'Kitchen', 12.00, 'active', 0, 'Popular item', '2026-02-11'),
  ('Wall Calendar', 'Decor', 6.50, 'active', 0, NULL, '2026-02-11');

quizDraft.title: Insert multiple rows with explicit column list
quizDraft.prompt:
Add two new items to inventory_items, but only specify the columns: name, category, and price. Use these values:

- 'Gift Card', 'Accessories', 25.00
- 'Mini Plant', 'Decor', 7.25

Write a single SQL statement to insert both rows. The other columns should use their default values.
quizDraft.hint: List only the three columns in the INSERT statement. The other columns will use defaults.

help.concept: If you omit columns in an INSERT, the database uses default values or NULL if allowed.
help.hint_1: Write INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price) VALUES (...), (...);
help.hint_2: Don't include columns like status, is_test, notes, or last_updated. Only name, category, and price.

quizDraft.starterCode: -- Insert both items with only name, category, and price
quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price)
VALUES
  ('Gift Card', 'Accessories', 25.00),
  ('Mini Plant', 'Decor', 7.25);

quizDraft.title: Which statements about inserting multiple rows are true?
quizDraft.prompt: Select all statements that are true about inserting multiple rows with a single INSERT statement.
quizDraft.hint: Think about syntax, efficiency, and column requirements.

help.concept: Inserting multiple rows at once is efficient and requires matching column order and types.
help.hint_1: Multiple rows can be inserted by separating value sets with commas.
help.hint_2: All value sets must match the column list, and this approach is more efficient than separate statements.

options: a. You can insert several rows at once by listing multiple value sets in the VALUES clause.
b. All rows must match the column order and types specified in the INSERT statement.
c. You must always specify every column in the table when inserting multiple rows.
d. Inserting multiple rows in one statement is more efficient than separate single-row inserts.
e. You cannot use NULL values when inserting multiple rows.

quizDraft.title: Fill in the blank: INSERT syntax
quizDraft.prompt:
Complete the following statement to insert two rows into inventory_items with only the name and price columns:

INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, price) VALUES ('Sample A', 10.00), (______, 12.50);
quizDraft.hint: What value should go in the blank to insert a row with name 'Sample B' and price 12.50?

help.concept: Each set of parentheses in VALUES represents a row. The values must match the columns listed.
help.hint_1: The blank should be filled with the name for the second row.
help.hint_2: Look for the value that matches the pattern: ('Sample B', 12.50).

quizDraft.template: INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, price) VALUES ('Sample A', 10.00), (______, 12.50);
